scenario: Quantum Retail fulfills 4500 orders per day in Camunda 8.8. Each order contains a list of line items, and a verify-line-item service task must run independently for every entry. The team wants every line-item invocation to be created at once and run in parallel rather than one after the other.
question: Which multi-instance configuration runs every iteration concurrently rather than one at a time?

options.text: Multi-instance marker set to parallel creates one job instance per element of the input collection and runs every iteration concurrently
Multi-instance marker set to sequential creates one job instance at a time and runs the next iteration only after the previous one completes
Multi-instance marker set to parallel runs iterations one after another while creating every iteration's job in advance for tracing purposes
Multi-instance marker set to sequential runs iterations concurrently and uses the input collection only to define the completion condition

a.text: Correct. Parallel multi-instance creates one instance per input element and runs all instances concurrently, fitting the per-line-item parallel pattern.

b.text: Incorrect. Sequential multi-instance runs one iteration at a time, which contradicts the scenario's parallel requirement.

c.text: Incorrect. The parallel marker actually runs iterations concurrently; serialising them defeats the purpose of the marker.

d.text: Incorrect. Sequential always runs iterations one at a time; the completion condition is a separate property, not a substitute for parallelism.

explanation: The parallel multi-instance marker creates one execution per input element and runs them concurrently, while the sequential marker runs iterations one at a time.
